evals.prompt: What authentication types can I use for connectors?
evals.expected_output: Lists all 5 connector auth types with descriptions: none, apikey, oauth_client_credentials, jwt, oauth_jwt_bearer. Mentions get_secret() for credentials.

assertions.name: lists-all-auth-types
assertions.text: Lists all 5 authentication types: none, apikey, oauth_client_credentials, jwt, oauth_jwt_bearer

assertions.name: mentions-get-secret
assertions.text: Mentions get_secret() as the way to handle credentials securely

evals.prompt: How do I query the DOM from inside a widget?
evals.expected_output: Explains Shadow DOM isolation, recommends sdk.$() and sdk.$$() instead of document.querySelector, mentions branding CSS custom properties.

assertions.name: explains-shadow-dom
assertions.text: Explains that widgets run inside Shadow DOM and standard DOM queries won't work

assertions.name: recommends-sdk-selectors
assertions.text: Recommends sdk.$() and sdk.$$() as replacements for document.querySelector and document.querySelectorAll

evals.prompt: What types can I use for configuration properties?
evals.expected_output: Lists all configuration property types: text, number, color, select, toggle, date, boolean. Explains how to access them at runtime.

assertions.name: lists-property-types
assertions.text: Lists the configuration property types including at least: text, number, color, select, toggle, boolean

assertions.name: explains-runtime-access
assertions.text: Explains how to access configuration values at runtime (sdk.getProps() or {{ property_name }} templates)

evals.prompt: How do page targeting rules work for scripts and stylesheets?
evals.expected_output: Explains the rules system with fields (authenticated, pageType, contentType), operators (eq, neq, in, nin), and default behavior.

assertions.name: explains-rule-fields
assertions.text: Describes the available rule fields: authenticated, pageType, and contentType

assertions.name: explains-operators
assertions.text: Lists the available operators: eq, neq, in, nin
